title: Community Shelters * Homeless Families - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Community Shelters * Homeless Families 32 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Ex-Offenders Homeless Couples Homeless Families Homeless Men Homeless Veterans Homeless Women Homeless Youth Mothers Pregnant Individuals Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness COMMUNITY ACTION WASHINGTON COUNTY COMMUNITY ACTION HILLSBORO FAMILY SHELTER HILLSBORO FAMILY SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a year-round shelter that can house families for up to five weeks. Tenant education is available to shelter residents. Residents meet regularly with a family advocate to work on goals, establish a financial plan, and find stable permanent housing. Also provides support for school-aged children to stay in their neighborhood schools. Emergency food boxes are available. 210 SE 12th Avenue Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Shelter: Families with minor children and pregnant women. Preference is given to families living on the street and women who are over 8 months pregnant. Food boxes: Unrestricted Hours: Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Waitlist: Must be referred by Community Connect Food box: Requests accepted Monday-Friday 5:30pm-9pm, Saturday/Sunday 9am-9pm Email (503) 640-3263 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BOISE RESCUE MISSION MINISTRIES CITY LIGHT HOME FOR WOMEN AND CHILDREN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers meals and emergency shelter. Shelter services include clothing provision, education, work-search assistance, mental health counseling, addiction recovery through the New Life Program, children’s programs, and coordination services with Boise Veteran's Affairs for homeless female veterans. Eligible community members will not be turned away for food or a clean bed due to lack of space. 1404 W Jefferson Street Boise, ID 83702 Eligibility: Women experiencing homelessness, women with children, and women who are veterans Hours: Shelter check in: 7 days per week 4pm Breakfast: Sunday-Friday 6am, Saturday 8am Lunch: 7 days per week noon Dinner: 7 days per week 5:30pm Email (208) 368-9901 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BOISE RESCUE MISSION MINISTRIES VALLEY WOMEN AND CHILDREN'S SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers overnight emergency shelter. Services at the shelter include meals, clothing, and case management. Boise Rescue Mission Ministries has a policy to never turn down anyone for food or shelter due to a lack of space. 869 W Corporate Lane Nampa, ID 83651 Eligibility: Women age 18 and older who are experiencing homelessness or have minor children Hours: Overnight shelter check-in: 7 days per week 4pm Email (208) 475-0725 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLATSOP COMMUNITY ACTION COLUMBIA INN EMERGENCY SHELTER COLUMBIA INN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a community shelter with up to 64 beds. 495 Marine Drive Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Families with children experiencing homelessness, individuals and families escaping domestic violence, and youth age 18-24 Prioritizes families with children, individuals with mobility-affecting disabilities, and individuals who are part of the Latinx, 2SLGBTQIA+, and Veteran communities Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 8:30am-3:30pm Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 325-1400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY PROMISE OF GREATER WASHINGTON COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a community shelter. Clients will have access to case management services with the goal of establishing self-sufficiency and eliminating other barriers to permanent housing. 5625 SW Erickson Avenue Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: Families with children age 0-18 and pregnant women. Individuals must agree to be drug and alcohol free upon entry and for the duration of the program. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (503) 640-3263 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY PROMISE OF LINCOLN COUNTY FAMILY SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shelter program with supportive services including access to a day center which provides a kitchen, laundry, showers, and a computer work station; case management services for support and resource navigation; and life skills, parenting, financial literacy, nutrition, and second class renters' courses. 5030 SE Highway 101 Lincoln City, OR 97367 Eligibility: Homeless and low-income families with children under age 18 or receiving services from Lincoln County School District and pregnant women. Prospective clients must be clean and sober, and pass a background check. Hours: Intake: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 614-0964 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY PROMISE OF METRO EAST FAMILY PROMISE OF METRO EAST FAMILY SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides shelter with case management services. Residents must undergo a background check, remain clean and sober throughout their stay, and be willing to participate in the organization's program to overcome barriers and apply for sustainable housing. Preference is given to families whose last housed location was in Multnomah County. 4837 NE Couch Street Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Homeless families with minor children. Does not accept clients fleeing domestic violence. Hours: Phone: Monday-Thursday 10am-5pm Email (503) 753-3960 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY PROMISE OF THE MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers short-term emergency shelter. Families receive case management and other housing resources, and work to create and follow a plan that builds self-sufficiency that leads toward permanent stability. The program's goal is to help families secure stable, permanent housing within 30 days. This is a faith-inspired program but does not require any affiliation, belief, or attendance at religious services. 1055 Edgewater Street NW Salem, OR 97304 Eligibility: Homeless families (individuals or couples with children under age 18). Pregnant individuals, when space is available. Parents are responsible for supervising their children and following program rules. Must not have active alcohol or drug use, though past use is reviewed on a case-by-case basis. All adults must agree to a criminal background check, with prior convictions considered individually. Hours: Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Day Center for clients: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 370-9752 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GOOD NEIGHBOR CENTER FAMILY SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a family shelter with active assessment and action plans, tenant education, and support. Families have up to 90 days to fulfill their plans and goals. Families have access to their own private rooms. The shelter is equipped with food from community donors for the guests to use to prepare their own meals daily. 11130 SW Greenburg Road Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Pregnant women and families with partial or full custody or guardianship of children under 18 years of age Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 640-3263 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GOSPEL RESCUE MISSION GOSPEL RESCUE MISSION FIKSO FAMILY CENTER WOMEN'S SHELTER WOMEN AND CHILDREN'S SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides shelter for women and children, subject to review every 30 days. Residents must attend morning and evening chapel and Sunday church services. 530 SW Foundry Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Single women and women with children age 17 and younger. No smoking or drug use allowed. No male visitors are allowed in the shelter at any time. Hours: Intake Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-noon Shelter hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 200-6242 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HELPING HANDS REENTRY OUTREACH CENTERS HELPING HANDS REENTRY BYBEE LAKES HOPE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency shelter and transitional housing. Participants have access to food and clothing, assistance with applying for health insurance, and a comprehensive needs assessment process supported by a case manager. Can provide information and referrals about identification replacement, schooling, and job placement services. 14355 N Bybee Lake Court Portland, OR 97203 Eligibility: -Adults age 18 and older and families with children under age 18 experiencing homelessness or are re-entering the community from incarceration -Clients must not have or be under the influence of any intoxicants on-site. Individuals with a registered sex offense are not eligible. Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Emergency Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days a week Email (971) 333-5070 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HELPING HANDS REENTRY OUTREACH CENTERS HELPING HANDS REENTRY CLATSOP COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency shelter and transitional housing. Participants have access to food and clothing, assistance with applying for health insurance, and a comprehensive needs assessment process supported by a case manager. Can provide information and referrals about identification replacement, schooling, and job placement services. 286 W Marine Drive Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: -Adults age 18 and older and families with children under age 18 experiencing homelessness or are re-entering the community from incarceration -Clients must not have or be under the influence of any intoxicants on-site. Individuals with a registered sex offense are not eligible. Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Emergency Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days a week Email (503) 739-4347 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HELPING HANDS REENTRY OUTREACH CENTERS HELPING HANDS REENTRY TILLAMOOK COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency shelter and transitional housing. Participants have access to food and clothing, assistance with applying for health insurance, and a comprehensive needs assessment process supported by a case manager. Can provide information and referrals about identification replacement, schooling, and job placement services. 6505 Headquarter Street Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: -Adults age 18 and older and families with children under age 18 experiencing homelessness or are re-entering the community from incarceration -Clients must not have or be under the influence of any intoxicants on-site. Individuals with a registered sex offense are not eligible. Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Emergency Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days a week Email (503) 354-8014 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JEFFERSON COUNTY FAITH BASED NETWORK MADRAS SHELTER SERVICE CENTER MADRAS SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 27-bed low-barrier overnight shelter with men’s, women’s and children’s, and wheelchair-accessible dorms, plus bathrooms, a community room, kitchen, and lockers. Offers meals for overnight guests and access to case management, showers, and laundry services. Volunteer opportunities are available. 61 NW Oak Street Madras, OR 97741 Eligibility: Adults age 18 and older and families with children. One pet per household. Hours: Shelter: 7 days per week 6pm-7am; check-in 6pm-10pm. Lunch: 7 days per week 11am-12:30pm Dinner: 7 days per week 6pm-7:30pm Showers: Monday/Friday 7:30am-12:30pm Laundry: Thursdays 7am-1pm drop off, Friday pick up times vary Email (458) 800-4208 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KLAMATH FALLS GOSPEL MISSION BERRY HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an emergency short term shelter. Guests will have access to meals, showers, and clothing. 1931 Mission Avenue Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Women with children age 0-10. Prospective guests must be clean and sober before intake Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-3:30pm Email (541) 882-4895 0 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LANE COUNTY HUMAN SERVICE DIVISION LANE COUNTY SHELTER FINDER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shelter locator with bed availability in Lane County. Not all shelters in Lane County are listed. 151 W 7th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Varies by shelter Hours: Not applicable Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIFEBOAT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a community shelter. Guests will be served a hot meal and have access to showers, laundry, toilietries, a clothing closet. phone and internet access, socks, and underwear. Maximum capacity is 22 individuals and beds are available on a first-come, first-serve basis. 1040 Commercial Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Adults age 18 and older, emancipated youth, and families with minor children Hours: Intake: 7 days per week 6pm-6am Check-in: 7 days per week 6pm-8pm Email (503) 468-8176 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEIGHBORIMPACT BEND OFFICE EMERGENCY SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a low-barrier, temporary shelter. The units provide private bedrooms and baths. Guests must meet weekly with a supportive services advocate. 20310 Empire Avenue Suite A100 Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Adults age 18 and older and families with children. Sobriety is not required; no substance use allowed on site. Hours: Intake line: Tuesdays and Thursdays noon-4pm Stepping Stone office hours: 7 days per week 8am-8pm Email (541) 548-2380 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PATH HOME FAMILY VILLAGE SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides 24/7 shelter in private, locked living and sleeping areas, meals, showers, laundry facilities and on-site supportive services and case management for families as they seek permanent housing. Eligibility: Families with children in their custody under the age of 18 (or plans for reunification) - OR - a third trimester pregnancy (28 weeks or 7 months) with connections to Multnomah County (i.e., was displaced, has kids in school, job, service providers in Multnomah County, etc.) AND meet one of the following criteria: -In an emergency shelter, transitional housing, motel paid for by an agency or voucher or a place not meant for habitation (i.e. car, streets, tent, abandoned building, etc.) -Doubled up (with family or friends and not on the lease, unsafely doubled up or in a living situation that is over safe capacity) and at risk of becoming literally homeless within 14 days -Housed, including self pay at a motel, and will become literally homeless within 14 days or has received a court ordered eviction notice -In an institution (i.e. jail, hospital, substance use or mental health treatment facility, etc.) and at risk of exiting to homelessness within 14 days -Fleeing or attempting to flee domestic violence and lacks the resources or support networks to obtain other safe, permanent housing HOUSEHOLDS ON A LEASE: Families who are currently on a lease and do not have an eviction notice are not eligible. Please seek rent payment assistance. YOUTH: Youth under 18 with children or pregnant are not eligible. Please seek assistance through the Coordinated Entry System for Youth. PETS: Pets allowed. The number and type of pets allowed vary by shelter. If you are offered a shelter space, the caseworker working with you will coordinate the shelter that best suits the family and their pets upon shelter placement. Hours: Callback List for Assessment: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Intake: scheduled by shelter provider after assessment when shelter space is available Shelter: 24/7 211 Get Directions Visit Website More Details REDEMPTION HOUSE MINISTRIES REDEMPTION HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transitional shelter with a maximum capacity of 17 individuals. A shower and load of laundry is offered for guests. Dinner and sack breakfast provided for people staying overnight. 780 E 1st Street Prineville, OR 97754 Eligibility: Women age 18 and older who are experiencing homelessness or have minor children Hours: Monday-Friday 4pm-8am Email (541) 362-5642 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROSEBURG RESCUE MISSION SAMARITAN INN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shelter with 110 beds including meals, showers, laundry facilities, and clothing. Children must be accompanied by a parent or legal guardian. 544 W Umpqua Street Roseburg, OR 97471 Eligibility: Women over age 18, pregnant women, and women with children (boys up to age 14). No pets accepted. Hours: Check in: 6pm for new residents; earlier arrangements can be made if referred by a social service organization, law enforcement, or medical provider Shelter curfew: 6:30pm Meals: Breakfast: Monday-Friday 7am-7:30am, Saturday-Sunday 8am-8:30am Lunch: Monday-Friday noon-12:30pm, Saturday-Sunday 1pm-1:30pm Dinner: 7 days per week 5pm-5:30pm (541) 672-5387 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEVERE WEATHER DESCHUTES COUNTY LA PINE WARMING SHELTER BY THE DOOR AT LA PINE OVERNIGHT WINTER WARMING SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offering overnight shelter throughout the winter season. 16430 3rd Street La Pine, OR 97739 Eligibility: Adults age 18 and older and families with children. Well behaved pets are welcome; pets must remain with owner. Hours: 7 days per week 5pm-7am, Monday, December 1, 2025-Sunday, March 15, 2026 -Meals served from 6pm-7pm Email (541) 948-6649 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEVERE WEATHER DESCHUTES COUNTY REDMOND WINTER SHELTER BY SHEPHERD'S HOUSE MINISTRIES OVERNIGHT WINTER WARMING SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offering overnight shelter throughout the winter season. A meal is served each night. To-go bags are given out each morning. 1350 South Highway 97 Redmond, OR 97756 Eligibility: Adults age 18 and older, families with minor children, and well behaved pets Hours: 7 days per week 6pm-7am Email (541) 388-2096 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEVERE WEATHER LINN COUNTY SWEET HOME POLICE DEPARTMENT OVERNIGHT WINTER WARMING SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers overnight shelter when temperatures are extremely cold. 1950 Main Street Sweet Home, OR 97386 Hours: Open 24 hours per day / 7 days per week when temperatures reach 32 degrees or below Email (541) 967-3950 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEVERE WEATHER LINN COUNTY THE RIVER CENTER OVERNIGHT WINTER WARMING SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers overnight shelter temperatures reach 32 degrees or below. Snacks will be available. 3000 S Santiam Highway Lebanon, OR 97355 Eligibility: Age 18 and older and families with minor children. Pets allowed. Hours: Open 7 days per week when temperatures reach 32 degrees or below 7pm-7am Shelter will close at 10pm if there are zero participants (541) 451-1271 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEVERE WEATHER UNION COUNTY RIGHT TRACK RESOURCE CENTER OVERNIGHT WINTER WARMING SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offering overnight shelter throughout the winter season. Hot meals, grab and go meals and snacks, and shower/laundry access will be provided. There is no co-ed sleeping. 501 3rd Street La Grande, OR 97850 Eligibility: Adults age 18 and older and families with minor children. Pets allowed but must be crated or leashed. Must not have a level 3 sex offense or arson conviction. Hours: 7 days per week 6pm-8am Saturday, November 15, 2025-Sunday, March 15, 2026 Intake: 6pm-10:30pm Doors are locked between 10:30pm-5am (541) 962-7994 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL MEDFORD ST ANTHONY EMERGENCY FAMILY SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a emergency shelter for families. 2424 N Pacific Highway Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: -Homeless families (single males with children, single females with children, and couples with children). No animals allowed. -Must be able to pass a criminal background check. Age 12 and older must be able to pass a drug test. No substance use allowed on site. Hours: Shelter: 7 days per week 4pm-10am Sign in: Monday-Friday 10am-11am Interviews: By appointment Email (541) 772-3828 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY FIRST PLACE FAMILY CENTER NIGHT SHELTER ANNEX Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a year-round community shelter that includes a wraparound case management program. Services include access to individual dorm units, laundry, showers, meals, and mail services. 4060 W Amazon Drive Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Families with children under age 18 and pregnant women (proof of pregnancy required) who are experiencing homelessness Hours: Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Intake: 7 days per week 9am-5pm (541) 342-7728 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNION GOSPEL MISSION OF SALEM SIMONKA PLACE FOR WOMEN AND CHILDREN SIMONKA PLACE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency shelter. Services available to guests include food, emergency clothing, laundry, mail services, faith-based addiction and mental health counseling, and case management. Guests may have the opportunity to move forward into the New Life Fellowship transitional housing program. Shelter availability may vary depending on the time of day. 5119 River Road N Keizer, OR 973030431 Eligibility: Women age 18 and older who are experiencing homelessness or have minor children (boys must be 17 years or younger). Service animals accepted. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 362-7487 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COUNTY GOSPEL RESCUE MISSION YAMHILL COUNTY GOSPEL RESCUE MISSION MEN'S FACILITY 30 DAY SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides 30-day short-term shelter. Services include a clothing closet, showers, and hygiene items. Shelter space is limited. Unaccompanied minors may be eligible for services with the permission of a guardian or law enforcement agency. 1234 NE 14th Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Adults age 18 and older and families with children. Guests must abstain from substance use for the duration of their stay. Animals are allowed. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week. Doors lock at 10pm. Email (503) 472-9766 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COUNTY GOSPEL RESCUE MISSION YAMHILL COUNTY GOSPEL RESCUE MISSION OVERNIGHT SHELTER OVERNIGHT SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an overnight emergency shelter. Dinner, breakfast, showers, laundry, and resource assistance will be provided to those staying at the shelter. Unaccompanied minors may be eligible for services with the permission of a guardian or law enforcement agency. 1315 NE Macy Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Age 18 and older and families with children. Guests may not use illegal drugs or alcohol during their stay. Animals are allowed. Hours: April-November Tuesday/Thursday 8pm-8am December-March 7 days per week 8pm-8am Email (503) 472-9766 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COUNTY GOSPEL RESCUE MISSION YAMHILL COUNTY GOSPEL RESCUE MISSION WOMEN'S FACILITY 30 DAY SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides 30-day short-term shelter. Services include a clothing closet, showers, and hygiene items. Shelter space is limited. Unaccompanied minors may be eligible for services with the permission of a guardian or law enforcement agency. 1340 NE Logan Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Adults age 18 and older and families with children. Guests must abstain from substance use for the duration of their stay. Animals are allowed. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week. Doors lock at 10pm. Email (503) 472-9766 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION WASHINGTON COUNTY HILLSBORO FAMILY SHELTER CLATSOP COMMUNITY ACTION COLUMBIA INN FAMILY PROMISE OF GREATER WASHINGTON COUNTY FAMILY PROMISE OF GREATER WASHINGTON COUNTY FAMILY PROMISE OF LINCOLN COUNTY FAMILY SHELTER FAMILY PROMISE OF METRO EAST FAMILY PROMISE OF METRO EAST FAMILY SHELTER FAMILY PROMISE OF THE MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY FAMILY PROMISE OF THE MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY GOOD NEIGHBOR CENTER FAMILY SHELTER GOSPEL RESCUE MISSION WOMEN AND CHILDREN'S SHELTER HELPING HANDS REENTRY OUTREACH CENTERS HELPING HANDS REENTRY BYBEE LAKES HOPE CENTER HELPING HANDS REENTRY OUTREACH CENTERS HELPING HANDS REENTRY CLATSOP COUNTY HELPING HANDS REENTRY OUTREACH CENTERS HELPING HANDS REENTRY TILLAMOOK COUNTY JEFFERSON COUNTY FAITH BASED NETWORK MADRAS SHELTER KLAMATH FALLS GOSPEL MISSION BERRY HOUSE LANE COUNTY HUMAN SERVICE DIVISION LANE COUNTY SHELTER FINDER LIFEBOAT SERVICES LIFEBOAT SERVICES NEIGHBORIMPACT EMERGENCY SHELTER REDEMPTION HOUSE MINISTRIES REDEMPTION HOUSE ROSEBURG RESCUE MISSION SAMARITAN INN SEVERE WEATHER DESCHUTES COUNTY OVERNIGHT WINTER WARMING SHELTER SEVERE WEATHER DESCHUTES COUNTY OVERNIGHT WINTER WARMING SHELTER SEVERE WEATHER LINN COUNTY OVERNIGHT WINTER WARMING SHELTER SEVERE WEATHER LINN COUNTY OVERNIGHT WINTER WARMING SHELTER SEVERE WEATHER UNION COUNTY OVERNIGHT WINTER WARMING SHELTER ST VINCENT DE PAUL MEDFORD ST ANTHONY EMERGENCY FAMILY SHELTER ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY NIGHT SHELTER ANNEX UNION GOSPEL MISSION OF SALEM SIMONKA PLACE YAMHILL COUNTY GOSPEL RESCUE MISSION 30 DAY SHELTER YAMHILL COUNTY GOSPEL RESCUE MISSION OVERNIGHT SHELTER YAMHILL COUNTY GOSPEL RESCUE MISSION 30 DAY SHELTER
